serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2019 [dataset]. Version 1. Pretoria: Statistics SA [producer], 2019. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2019. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/vtvj-pv21

verStmt.version: v1.0: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: Version 1 was originally downloaded from Stats SA in February 2020

stdyInfo.abstract:
The GHS is an annual household survey which measures the living circumstances of South African households. The GHS collects data on education, health, and social development, housing, access to services and facilities, food security, and agriculture.
Note: The questionnaire for the GHS series changed in 2019 and the variables were also renamed. See the document ghs-2019-variables-renamed for a correspondence between the old names and the new ones.

sumDscr.geogCover: The General Household Survey has national coverage.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation for the data is Province (and metropolitan municipality, where this applies)
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa, and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as student hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons, and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes:
The scope of the General Household Survey includes:
Household characteristics: Dwelling type, home ownership, access to water and sanitation, access to services, transport, household assets, land ownership, agricultural production Individuals' characteristics: demographic characteristics, relationship to household head, marital status, language, education, employment, income, health, fertility, mortality, disability, access to social services

dataColl.sampProc:
From 2015 the General Household Survey (GHS) uses a Master Sample (MS) frame developed in 2013 as a general-purpose sampling frame to be used for all Stats SA household-based surveys. This MS has design requirements that are reasonably compatible with the GHS. The 2013 Master Sample is based on information collected during the 2011 Census conducted by Stats SA. In preparation for Census 2011, the country was divided into 103 576 enumeration areas (EAs). The census EAs, together with the auxiliary information for the EAs, were used as the frame units or building blocks for the formation of primary sampling units (PSUs) for the Master Sample, since they covered the entire country, and had other information that is crucial for stratification and creation of PSUs. There are 3 324 primary sampling units (PSUs) in the Master Sample, with an expected sample of approximately 33 000 dwelling units (DUs). The number of PSUs in the current Master Sample (3 324) reflect an 8,0% increase in the size of the Master Sample compared to the previous (2008) Master Sample (which had 3 080 PSUs). The larger Master Sample of PSUs was selected to improve the precision (smaller coefficients of variation, known as CVs) of the GHS estimates. The Master Sample is designed to be representative at provincial level and within provinces at metro/non-metro levels. Within the metros, the sample is further distributed by geographical type. The three geography types are Urban, Tribal and Farms. This implies, for example, that within a metropolitan area, the sample is representative of the different geography types that may exist within that metro.
The sample for the GHS is based on a stratified two-stage design with probability proportional to size (PPS) sampling of PSUs in the first stage, and sampling of dwelling units (DUs) with systematic sampling in the second stage.After allocating the sample to the provinces, the sample was further stratified by geography (primary stratification), and by population attributes using Census 2011 data (secondary stratification).
dataColl.resInstru: Stats SA transitioned to electronic data collection in 2019, and the GHS was redesigned to be captured using computer assisted personal interviews. Some of the variables were also renamed. See the document ghs-2019-variables-renamed for a correspondence between the old names and the new ones.
dataColl.weight:
The sample weights were constructed in order to account for the following: the original selection probabilities (design weights), adjustments for PSUs that were sub-sampled or segmented, excluded population from the sampling frame, non-response, weight trimming, and benchmarking to known population estimates from the Demographic Analysis Division within Stats SA.
The sampling weights for the data collected from the sampled households were constructed so that the responses could be properly expanded to represent the entire civilian population of South Africa. The design weights, which are the inverse sampling rate (ISR) for the province, are assigned to each of the households in a province.
Mid-year population estimates produced by the Demographic Analysis Division were used for benchmarking. The final survey weights were constructed using regression estimation to calibrate to national level population estimates cross-classified by 5-year age groups, gender and race, and provincial population estimates by broad age groups. The 5-year age groups are: 0–4, 5–9, 10–14, 55–59, 60–64; and 65 and over. The provincial level age groups are 0–14, 15–34, 35–64; and 65 years and over. The calibrated weights were constructed such that all persons in a household would have the same final weight.
The Statistics Canada software StatMx was used for constructing calibration weights. The population controls at national and provincial level were used for the cells defined by cross-classification of Age by Gender by Race. Records for which the age, population group or sex had item non-response could not be weighted and were therefore excluded from the dataset. No additional imputation was done to retain these records.
Household estimates that were developed using the UN headship ratio methodology were used to weight household files. The databases of Census 1996, Census 2001, Community Survey 2007 Census 2011 were used to analyse trends and develop models to predict the number of households for each year. The weighting system was based on tables for the expected distribution of household heads for specific age categories, per population group and province.

anlyInfo.dataAppr: Statistics South Africa removed the EDU_SAME variable from the public release of the GHS 2019 data because a coding error in the electronic questionnaire meant the data was not reliable. The coding error was in the enabling condition (skip instruction) for EDU21 which meant that most of the respondents who would otherwise have answered the question on whether they were doing the same grade as the year before were not asked the question. Once identified the critical error was corrected in subsequent questionnaires.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2019 [dataset]. Version 1. Pretoria: Statistics SA [producer], 2019. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2019. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/vtvj-pv21
useStmt.conditions: Public access data, available to all

catgry.labl: Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on a farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on a farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat

var.name: HSG_ROOF
var.labl: Type of material used for the roof

var.name: HSG_WALLS
var.labl: Type of material used for the walls

var.name: HSG_FLOOR
var.labl: Type of material used for the floor

var.name: HSG_RM_OPEN
var.labl: Number of open-plan dining rooms/sitting rooms/TV rooms

catgry.labl: Owned, but not yet paid off to bank/financial institution
Rented from other (including municipality and social housing institutions)
Owned, but not yet paid off to private lender
Piped (tap) water on site or in yard

var.name: WAT_DIST
var.labl: Distance of water source from the dwelling

var.name: WAT_PIPE
var.labl: Usage of piped or tap water

var.name: WAT_YNOTPAY
var.labl: Reason household not paying for water

catgry.labl: Pay directly to landlord as part of rent
Permission from municipality not to pay
Flush toilet connected to a public sewerage system
Flush toilet connected to a septic tank or conservancy tank
Pour bucket-flush toilet connected to a septic tank (or septic pit)
Open defecation (e.g. no facilities, field, bush)

var.name: SAN_WASH
var.labl: Use of soap to wash hands after using toilet

catgry.labl: Clean hands with hand sanitiser or wet wipes
Connected to other source which the household pays for (e.g. connected to neighbour's line and paying neighbours)
Connected to other source which the household does not pay for (e.g. connected to neighbour's line and not paying)
Removed by local authority/private company/community members at least once a week
Removed by local authority/private company/community members less often than once a week

var.name: SWR_WILLPAYR
var.labl: Payment for the removal of refuse or rubbish

catgry.labl: Yes, recyclables are set out for kerbside collection by service providers / recycling companies
Yes, recyclables are taken to a drop-off point
Yes, recyclables are separated and placed in the household bin to support the waste pickers
No, but waste pickers sort through my bin

var.name: COM_CELL_TOTAL
var.labl: Total number of cellular telephones in the household

var.name: COM_Int_Libr
var.labl: Internet in a library or community hall/Thusong centre

var.name: COM_Int_stud
var.labl: Internet for students at a school/university/college

var.name: COM_Int_Cafe
var.labl: Internet café 2 km or less from the household

var.name: COM_Int_Cafe2
var.labl: Internet café more than 2 km from the household

var.name: COM_Int_Phone
var.labl: Any place via a mobile cellular telephone

var.name: COM_Int_Mobile
var.labl: Any place via any other mobile access services

catgry.labl: Delivered to a post box/private bag
Through friend or neighbour or relative

var.name: TRA_TAXI_DIST
var.labl: Distance to the nearest taxi stop

var.name: TRA_BUS_DIST
var.labl: Distance to the nearest bus stop

var.name: TRA_TRAIN_DIST
var.labl: Distance to the nearest train station

var.name: HHW_TRANSP
var.labl: Means of transport to nearest health facility

var.name: HHW_YNOTN
var.labl: Reason for not using nearest facility

catgry.labl: Drugs that were needed, not available
Staff rude or uncaring or turned patient away
Not on medical aid scheme list of facilities
Prefer to use a state/provincial health institution
Prefer to use a private health institution

var.name: FSD_FEWFOODS_DAYS
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: FSD_SKIPPED_DAYS
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: FSD_ATELESS_DAYS
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: FSD_RANOUT_DAYS
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: AGR_Agri_type_fod
var.labl: Fodder grazing/pasture or grass for animals

var.name: AGR_MainUse
var.labl: Use of agricultural products and stock keeping

catgry.labl: As a main source of food for the household
As the main source of income/earning a living
As an extra source of income
As an extra source of food for the household
As a leisure activity or hobby, e.g. gardening

var.name: AGR_Sell
var.labl: Agricultural products sold by the households

var.name: AGR_SellTo
var.labl: Buyers of the agricultural products sold

catgry.labl: Buyers from neighbouring cities and towns

var.name: AGR_Cattle
var.labl: The number Cattle the household owns

var.name: AGR_Sheep
var.labl: The number Sheep the household owns

var.name: AGR_Goats
var.labl: The number Goats the household owns

var.name: AGR_Pigs
var.labl: The number Pigs the household owns

var.name: AGR_Chickens
var.labl: The number Chickens the household owns

var.name: AGR_PLANT_vrd
var.labl: On verges of roads and unused public/municipal land

var.name: AGR_LANDSIZE
var.labl: Size of the land that the household uses

catgry.labl: Less than 5 000 m2 (approximately one soccer field)
5 000 m2 to 9 999 m2 (between one soccer field and one hectare)

var.name: FIN_INC_agric
var.labl: Sales of farm products and services

var.name: FIN_INC_oth
var.labl: Other income sources, e.g. rental income, interest

catgry.labl: Sales of farm products and services
Other income sources, e.g. rental income, interest

var.name: FIN_REM
var.labl: Approximately how much comes from remittances

var.name: FIN_REQINC
var.labl: Net household income per month in rand

var.name: FIN_COMPINC
var.labl: Monthly income of your household higher

var.name: HWL_DOMW
var.labl: Domestic or household worker's services used by the household

var.name: HWL_STATUS
var.labl: Wealth/poverty status of you and your household at present

var.name: HWL_HAPPY
var.labl: Happier, the same or less happy with life

var.name: HWL_Assets_rainwtnk
var.labl: Rain water tank / rain water harvesting system

var.name: HWL_RES_NAME
var.labl: Person who answered most of the questions in Section 5–8

var.name: psu
var.labl: Primary sampling unit Primary sampling unit Primary sampling unit

catgry.labl: Other relative (e.g. in-laws or aunt/uncle) of household head
Living together like husband and wife/partners
Single, but have lived together with someone as husband/wife before
Single and have never been married/never lived together as husband/wife before
Grade 12/Standard 10/Form 5/National Senior Certificate/Matric/ NCV Level 4/Occupational Certificate – NQF Level 4
Higher/National/Advance certificate with Grade 12/Std 10/Occupational Certificate – NQF Level 5
Diploma with Grade 12/Standard 10/Occupational Certificate – NQF Level 6
Higher Diploma/Occupational Certificate (B-Tech Diploma) – NQF Level 7
Honours Degree/Postgraduate Diploma/Occupational Certificate – NQF Level 8
Post Higher Diploma (M-Tech and Master's Degree) – NQF Level 9
Doctoral Degrees (D-Tech and PhD) – NQF Level 10

var.name: ECD_NOTATT
var.labl: Place where children spent most of their time during the day

catgry.labl: At home with parent, foster parent or guardian
At home with someone younger than 18 years

var.name: ECD_NOTATT_RSN
var.labl: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility

catgry.labl: Prefer that the child stay at home/with someone else
These facilities do not exist in our area

var.name: ECD_FEES
var.labl: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility

catgry.labl: Has completed education/satisfied with my level of education/do not want to study
He or she is working at home or business/job
Education is useless or not interesting
Pre-school (including ECD centre, e.g. daycare, crèche, playground, nursery or school pre-primary)
School (including Grade R to Grade 12 learners who attended a formal school)
Adult Education and Training Learning Centre (ABET/AET Centre)
Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) college
Other than any of the above
Minibus/bus provided by institution/government and not paid for
Vehicle hired by a group of parents
Own car or other private vehicle

var.name: EDU_TIME
var.labl: Time taken to travel to school

var.name: EDU_NOTNEAR
var.labl: Reason for not attending the nearest institution

var.name: EDU_TOTFEES
var.labl: Total amount of tuition fees paid

catgry.labl: No fee school (School does not ask for tuition fees)
got a bursary covering all costs

var.name: EDU_PROB_TABS
var.labl: Teachers are often absent from school

var.name: EDU_PROB_TSTR
var.labl: Teachers were involved in a strike

catgry.labl: Yes, a few times a week

var.name: EDU_VLNC
var.labl: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse

catgry.labl: Not applicable - school closed, e.g. school holiday
Did not want to go to school
Need to take care of someone else at home
Does not feel safe at school

var.name: HLT_ILLNESS_FLU
var.labl: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection

var.name: HLT_CONSULT
var.labl: Consult a health worker as a result of illness

var.name: HLT_CONSULT_YNOT
var.labl: Reason for not consulting a health worker

catgry.labl: Not necessary/the problem was not serious enough
Experienced difficulty getting a diagnosis before

var.name: HLT_INJPOIS_STVIOL
var.labl: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating

var.name: HLT_days_MISS
var.labl: Days missed at school or work

catgry.labl: Does not go to school or work
The child has been born alive
The child died in the womb or during childbirth after the 7th month of pregnancy (stillbirth)
The child died in the womb or the pregnancy ended before the 7th month of pregnancy (spontaneous abortion/miscarriage)
The pregnancy was ended by choice before the child was born (termination of pregnancy/abortion by choice)
Unable to do/cannot do at all
Unable to do/cannot do at all
Unable to do/cannot do at all
Unable to do/cannot do at all
Unable to do/cannot do at all
Unable to do/cannot do at all

var.name: LAB_WGE
var.labl: Working for a wage, commission or salary

catgry.labl: Lift club by a group of people sharing a private vehicle

var.name: LAB_MINS
var.labl: Time taken to place of employment

catgry.labl: In the formal sector (registered to perform activity)
In the informal sector (not registered to perform activity)

var.name: LAB_LOOK
var.labl: Looking for any kind of job or trying business

var.name: LAB_GOVPROG
var.labl: Participated in government or municipal job creation programme

var.name: LAB_RES_NAME
var.labl: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
